feed.title: PQC: Google's result on improving Shor's algorithm reconstructed

entry.title: PQC: Google's result on improving Shor's algorithm reconstructed
entry.summary:
The blog post discusses a significant advancement in the field of elliptic curve cryptography as detailed in a recent publication and accompanying research. The paper, available at [arXiv](https://arxiv.org/pdf/2606.02235), introduces new methodologies for point addition on elliptic curves, which is fundamental to cryptographic protocols such as encryption, digital signatures, and key exchanges. These enhanced techniques aim to improve both the security and efficiency of data encryption methods.
Additionally, the post includes a link to the source code associated with the research, hosted on GitLab. For those interested in exploring the practical application or further development of these techniques, the code can be accessed at [GitLab repository](https://gitlab.inria.fr/capsule/qarton-projects/ec-point-addition). This open-source resource provides a hands-on opportunity for other programmers and researchers to test, modify, and implement the algorithms discussed in the paper.
This research not only contributes to the theoretical underpinnings of cryptographic practices but also offers tangible tools for enhancing security measures across various digital platforms. By making the code available, the researchers encourage collaborative development and real-world testing, which could lead to broader adoption and optimization of these advanced cryptographic techniques.
